source_pr.title: support constant propagation
source_pr.body:
@shenli @hanfei1991 @zimulala @coocood @tiancaiamao 
PTAL
A transformation takes place for expressions like this:

> WHERE column1 = column2 AND column2 = 'x'

the transformed condition becomes:

> WHERE column1 = 'x' AND column2 = 'x'

With constant propagation, we can improve predicate push down
like:

> SELECT \* FROM t1 join t2 on t1.a = t2.a where t1.a = 1;

after propagate constant, we get 

> SELECT \* FROM t1 join t2 where t2.a = 1 and t1.a = 1;

and we can push down all the conditions.

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it compared a list of predicate/condition strings whose order was nondeterministic (e.g., derived from map iteration / optimizer output ordering). This PR stabilizes it by sorting the collected strings before joining and asserting, making the assertion deterministic.
fix_pattern: Sort collected results/strings before assertions to avoid nondeterministic ordering (e.g., from map iteration).

evidence: files: expression/expression.go, plan/predicate_push_down.go, plan/plan_test.go, executor/builder.go, plan/expression_rewriter.go, plan/logical_plan_builder.go
